title: Black Hole Evolution
definition: The transformation and eventual fate of stars, culminating in the formation of black holes.

difficulty_levels.beginner: When massive stars run out of fuel, they collapse under their own gravity, forming black holes that can't be seen or escaped from, except through the remarkable technology called the Event Horizon Telescope.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: Black hole evolution refers to the life cycle of massive stars that eventually collapse into singularities, creating regions of spacetime where gravity is so strong that not even light can escape.
difficulty_levels.expert: The process of black hole evolution reveals fascinating insights about general relativity, quantum gravity, and galaxy formation, providing a glimpse into the cosmic mysteries that underlie our universe's development.

extra_fields.insight: Black holes are the remnants of massive stars, marking significant milestones in celestial evolution.
